title: Standard Tiptap extensions (heading, paragraph, bold/italic, lists, code block)
description: Add the deliberate set of standard Tiptap extensions matching the MVP's block type list: heading, paragraph (default), bold, italic, ordered/unordered lists, and code block.
acceptanceCriteria: Editor supports headings (at least levels 1-3) via toolbar button or markdown shortcut (`#`, `##`, `###`)
Editor supports bold and italic via toolbar buttons and standard keyboard shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl+B, Cmd/Ctrl+I)
Editor supports ordered and unordered lists via toolbar buttons and markdown shortcuts (`-`/`1.`)
Editor supports fenced code blocks with a toolbar button and triple-backtick shortcut
A minimal toolbar UI exposes all the above actions with active-state highlighting reflecting the current selection's formatting

steps.details: Add `@tiptap/extension-code-block` (or `code-block-lowlight` if syntax highlighting is desired; plain `code-block` is sufficient for MVP).

Create `apps/admin/src/features/editor/EditorToolbar.tsx` with buttons for heading levels, bold, italic, lists, code block, each calling the corresponding `editor.chain().focus().toggleX().run()` and reflecting `editor.isActive('x')` for active state.

Using Playwright: type `# Heading` and confirm it becomes an H1; select text and click Bold, confirm it renders bold; use the toolbar for a code block and confirm the resulting JSON has `type: 'codeBlock'` via `editor.getJSON()`.

technicalNotes: This extension set intentionally excludes strike-through, underline, blockquote, and horizontal rule since they are not in the MVP's block type list from the PRD — add them later only if the block schema (TASK-3) is extended to match.
